payload.answer: recall returned mem_new because it is the current, non-superseded memory; mem_old was filtered as it is explicitly marked as superseded by mem_new in the system's version history.
citations: mem_new: Sam works at Goldman Sachs as a senior analyst. (timestamp: 2024-06-15)
mem_old: Sam previously worked at Goldman Sachs as an associate. (timestamp: 2022-03-10, status: superseded by mem_new)
payload.decision: The system recalled mem_new for Sam from Goldman because it is the most recent and non-superseded version, while mem_old was filtered out as it has been explicitly superseded by mem_new and marked as deprecated in the memory system.

entity_resolution.reason: The entity 'Sam' from Goldman was uniquely resolved using contextual employment history and temporal disambiguation; no conflicting entities were found.

memory_cards.statement: Sam works at Goldman Sachs as a senior analyst.
Sam previously worked at Goldman Sachs as an associate.

details: Superseded memory (mem_old) was filtered per system policy.
Entity resolution was unambiguous and consistent.
No conflicting or ambiguous facts were present in the active memory set.

repair_options: Verify if mem_old should be reinstated if the supersession was in error.
Check audit logs to confirm the supersession event and timestamp.
Allow user to override recall filtering with a 'show deprecated' flag.
